findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Password
findings.description: The product contains a hard-coded password, which it uses for its own inbound authentication or for outbound communication to external components.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/application.properties and application-docker.properties contain hard-coded database credentials: spring.datasource.password=photoalbum

findings.name: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
findings.description: The product allows the upload or transfer of dangerous file types that are automatically processed within its environment.
findings.evidence: PhotoServiceImpl.java:80 - File type validation relies solely on file.getContentType() (HTTP Content-Type header), which is user-controlled and can be spoofed. No magic byte or file content validation is performed.

findings.name: Use of Obsolete Function
findings.description: The code uses deprecated or obsolete functions, which suggests that the code has not been actively reviewed or maintained.
findings.evidence: Photo.java:5-8 - Uses deprecated javax.persistence.* and javax.validation.* APIs (old Java EE namespaces). Project uses Java 8 (java.version=1.8) and Spring Boot 2.7.18, both end-of-life/nearing EOL. javax.* namespaces are obsolete and replaced by jakarta.* in Java EE 9+ / Spring Boot 3.x.

findings.name: Use of Hard-coded Credentials
findings.description: The product contains hard-coded credentials, such as a password or cryptographic key.
findings.evidence: src/main/resources/application.properties:13-14 and application-docker.properties:3-4 contain hard-coded database credentials: spring.datasource.username=photoalbum, spring.datasource.password=photoalbum
